metadata.subject: English Language Arts
metadata.description: Composition 12 focuses on advanced writing across multiple forms and purposes, with emphasis on expository, analytical, and persuasive writing. Students develop sophisticated argumentation skills, conduct rigorous research, and learn to adapt their writing to different audiences and rhetorical situations. Through intensive study of style, voice, and revision, students strengthen their abilities to communicate complex ideas clearly and persuasively. This course prepares students for post-secondary writing demands and develops critical thinking through the writing process.
metadata.general_description: An advanced exploration of composition across multiple forms, emphasizing argumentation, research, style development, and the writing process.

big_ideas.statement: Effective writing is grounded in clear thinking about purpose, audience, and rhetorical situation, requiring writers to make deliberate choices about form and content.
big_ideas.elaboration: Successful writers analyze the context in which they are writing—who their audience is, what they want to accomplish, what genre conventions apply—and shape their writing accordingly. Understanding rhetorical situation enables writers to make strategic choices about argument, evidence, tone, and structure that maximize their persuasive or communicative impact.

big_ideas.statement: Argumentation and persuasion require sophisticated understanding of evidence, counterargument, logical reasoning, and ethical communication.
big_ideas.elaboration: Effective persuasive writing relies on credible evidence, fair representation of opposing views, logical reasoning, and clear explanation of why claims matter. Writers must understand different types of evidence, recognize logical fallacies, anticipate objections, and construct arguments that are both compelling and intellectually honest. Ethical persuasion acknowledges complexity and avoids manipulation.

big_ideas.statement: Research writing requires locating, evaluating, synthesizing, and integrating information from multiple sources to develop original thinking and well-supported claims.
big_ideas.elaboration: Research is not merely collecting information but actively engaging with sources to develop understanding and arguments. Strong research writers evaluate source credibility, synthesize ideas from multiple sources, integrate evidence appropriately, acknowledge their sources, and develop original analysis. Research contributes to critical thinking and informed citizenship.

big_ideas.statement: Style and voice emerge through deliberate craft choices about word choice, syntax, imagery, and tone; developing distinctive voice requires understanding and practice.
big_ideas.elaboration: Writers can develop more effective and distinctive styles by attending to how word choice shapes meaning, how sentence structure affects pace and emphasis, and how tone conveys attitude and establishes relationship with readers. Studying published writers' styles and practicing revision develop writers' abilities to craft language that serves their purposes and reflects their voices.

categories.name: Comprehend and Connect
categories.raw: Students understand writing as purposeful communication shaped by rhetorical situation; they analyze published writing to understand compositional strategies.

competencies.raw: Analyze how writers make choices about form, structure, and style in response to rhetorical situation
i_can_statements: I can identify the purpose, audience, and context for various texts
I can analyze how writers adapt their approach—tone, structure, evidence—to different audiences and situations
I can explain how genre conventions shape what writers do in particular writing forms
I can identify and discuss stylistic choices and their effects on readers

competencies.raw: Understand effective argumentation, including how writers develop claims, support arguments with evidence, and address counterarguments
i_can_statements: I can identify thesis statements and supporting arguments in persuasive texts
I can evaluate the quality and relevance of evidence used in arguments
I can recognize logical fallacies and assess their impact on argument strength
I can analyze how writers acknowledge and respond to opposing viewpoints

competencies.raw: Analyze how writers integrate research, sources, and evidence to develop well-supported writing
i_can_statements: I can identify how writers use different types of evidence and source material
I can evaluate the credibility and relevance of sources
I can analyze how writers synthesize information from multiple sources
I can examine how writers acknowledge sources and maintain academic integrity

categories.name: Create and Communicate
categories.raw: Students write purposefully across multiple forms and contexts, developing sophisticated argumentation, conducting research, and refining style and voice.

competencies.raw: Develop clear, well-supported thesis statements and construct coherent, logical arguments
i_can_statements: I can write focused thesis statements that respond to writing prompts or questions
I can organize ideas logically and ensure each paragraph supports my overall argument
I can provide specific, relevant evidence that supports my claims
I can explain the significance and implications of my argument

competencies.raw: Conduct research, locate and evaluate sources, and integrate research into writing appropriately
i_can_statements: I can identify reliable sources appropriate for my research questions
I can evaluate sources for credibility, bias, and relevance
I can take useful notes and organize research findings
I can integrate quotations, paraphrases, and summaries smoothly into my writing with proper citations

competencies.raw: Develop distinctive voice and style through deliberate word choice, syntax, and tone
i_can_statements: I can make strategic word choices that enhance clarity and impact
I can vary sentence structure to control pace and emphasis
I can adopt appropriate tone for different writing situations
I can develop more distinctive style through reading, practice, and revision

competencies.raw: Revise and edit extensively to strengthen clarity, coherence, argumentation, and correctness
i_can_statements: I can revise for coherence and logical flow of ideas
I can strengthen arguments and evidence through substantial revision
I can edit for sentence-level clarity and effectiveness
I can proofread carefully for grammar, mechanics, and formatting conventions

areas.name: Rhetorical Situation and Purpose
areas.raw: Understanding of how purpose, audience, genre, and context shape writing choices; analysis of how writers adapt to different rhetorical situations
topics: Identifying purpose, audience, and context in writing situations
Genre conventions and expectations in different writing forms
Tone, formality, and audience awareness in writing
Adapting structure and content to suit rhetorical situation
Writing for different purposes: persuasion, explanation, analysis, reflection
Analyzing how professional writers shape their writing for specific contexts

areas.name: Argumentation and Persuasion
areas.raw: Development of sophisticated skills in constructing arguments, supporting claims with evidence, understanding counterargument, and evaluating logical reasoning
topics: Thesis development and claim construction
Types of evidence and their effectiveness
Logical reasoning and avoiding logical fallacies
Addressing counterargument and objections
Building credibility and ethos in persuasive writing
Ethical considerations in persuasion and argumentation

areas.name: Research and Integration of Sources
areas.raw: Skills in locating, evaluating, and synthesizing research; integration of sources into writing; understanding academic integrity and citation conventions
topics: Developing research questions and search strategies
Evaluating source credibility, bias, and reliability
Locating primary and secondary sources
Synthesizing information from multiple sources
Integrating quotations, paraphrases, and summaries
Citation conventions and maintaining academic integrity

areas.name: Style, Voice, and Revision
areas.raw: Development of distinctive voice and effective style through study of craft, practice with sentence combining and revision, and analysis of published writing
topics: Word choice, diction, and connotation
Sentence structure and syntax for emphasis and clarity
Tone and voice in different writing contexts
Figurative language and imagery in expository writing
Paragraph structure and coherence
Revision strategies for strengthening clarity, argumentation, and style
